milestones.title: M0 Foundations
milestones.description: AWS foundation, environment separation, and release boundaries.

milestones.title: M1 Market State
milestones.description: Canonical market, book, order, and account state.

milestones.title: M2 Trade Core
milestones.description: Allocator, risk kernel, execution, and reconciliation.

milestones.title: M3 Control Plane

milestones.title: M4 Paper Readiness

issues.title: [Epic] M0 Foundations and environment separation
issues.body:
## Outcome
Establish the AWS, environment, and release boundaries required to build the v1 system without mixing nonprod and prod concerns.

## Scope
- Stand up the base AWS shape for `poly-nonprod` and `poly-prod`
- Freeze service naming, account separation, and release boundaries
- Define the secure path for secrets, config, and privileged deploy actions

## Acceptance Criteria
- `sim`, `paper`, and `prod` environment boundaries match the v1 environment matrix
- Infrastructure decisions support `openclaw-control`, `market-state`, and `trade-core`
- Production deploys require an explicit release action
- Nonprod cannot access prod secrets or credentials

## References
- `docs/specs/system-spec-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/environment-matrix-v1.md`

issues.title: Provision AWS foundation for nonprod and prod
issues.body:
## Outcome
Provision the AWS baseline for implementation and deployment.

## Scope
- Create or configure the nonprod and prod AWS accounts
- Create ECS clusters, ECR repositories, CloudWatch log groups, and Secrets Manager namespaces
- Define IAM roles for service tasks and deployment workflows

## Acceptance Criteria
- Both accounts exist and are reachable by the deployment workflow
- Service roles follow least privilege
- Shared naming and tagging conventions are documented
- Base resources are ready for `openclaw-control`, `market-state`, and `trade-core`

## References
- `docs/specs/system-spec-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/environment-matrix-v1.md`

issues.title: Implement environment config, secrets boundaries, and release gates
issues.body:
## Outcome
Make environment separation enforceable in code and deployment configuration.

## Scope
- Define environment-specific config loading
- Ensure `TRADING_ENABLED=false` by default
- Require explicit privileged action for any `prod` enablement
- Separate OpenAI, Slack, and Polymarket secrets by environment

## Acceptance Criteria
- Services fail closed if the environment is ambiguous
- Default runtime mode is `paper`
- Production secrets are inaccessible from nonprod tasks
- Release gating rules match the environment matrix

## References
- `docs/specs/environment-matrix-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/risk-policy-v1.md`

issues.title: [Epic] M1 Market-state service
issues.body:
## Outcome
Build the canonical market/account state service that all downstream components depend on.

## Scope
- Ingest tradable market discovery data
- Normalize live books, open orders, fills, balances, and positions
- Publish canonical state snapshots and health signals

## Acceptance Criteria
- `market-state` is the single source of truth for downstream services
- State can be marked stale and block new execution
- Canonical snapshots conform to the v1 service contracts

## References
- `docs/specs/system-spec-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/service-contracts-v1.md`

issues.title: Implement Polymarket market discovery and tradable universe ingestion
issues.body:
## Outcome
Continuously ingest the tradable Polymarket universe and prepare it for downstream filtering.

## Scope
- Pull market discovery data from Polymarket endpoints
- Normalize market identifiers, contract identifiers, and resolution metadata
- Tag markets into internal `market_complex_id` groups when known

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tradable market records are stored in a normalized format
- Closed or ineligible markets are filtered correctly
- Each tracked contract can be linked back to a market and market complex

## References
- `docs/specs/system-spec-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/service-contracts-v1.md`

issues.title: Implement real-time book, order, and account state normalization
issues.body:
## Outcome
Maintain a normalized real-time view of books, orders, balances, and positions.

## Scope
- Consume streaming or polled market/book data
- Model open orders, fills, balances, and positions in a single state layer
- Apply staleness timers and state health markers

## Acceptance Criteria
- Top-of-book and spread data are available for tracked contracts
- Position and order state reconcile into a coherent account view
- Stale state is detectable using the thresholds in the risk policy

## References
- `docs/specs/risk-policy-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/service-contracts-v1.md`

issues.title: Persist canonical snapshots and publish state events
issues.body:
## Outcome
Persist the canonical state and expose it to the rest of the system through the approved contracts.

## Scope
- Emit `market_snapshot` and `position_snapshot` events
- Store snapshots and health metadata in DynamoDB/S3 as appropriate
- Make the state available for replay and daily summaries

## Acceptance Criteria
- Snapshot payloads follow the v1 service contracts
- Event emission is traceable and versioned
- Stored state is usable by replay and scorecard workflows

## References
- `docs/specs/service-contracts-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/system-spec-v1.md`

issues.title: [Epic] M2 Trade-core service
issues.body:
## Outcome
Build the deterministic core that turns approved opportunities into safe order behavior.

## Scope
- Normalize proposals
- Rank and allocate capital
- Enforce risk policy
- Execute and reconcile orders

## Acceptance Criteria
- `trade-core` owns allocator, risk, execution, and reconciliation responsibilities
- Risk checks cannot be bypassed by LLM output
- Execution follows intents rather than direct LLM order instructions

## References
- `docs/specs/system-spec-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/risk-policy-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/service-contracts-v1.md`

issues.title: Implement proposal normalization and portfolio allocator
issues.body:
## Outcome
Rank standardized opportunities across sleeves and produce allocator decisions.

## Scope
- Validate incoming `strategy_proposal` payloads
- Rank opportunities by edge, confidence, and portfolio fit
- Produce `allocator_decision` records with traceable sizing

## Acceptance Criteria
- Proposals are rejected if required fields are missing
- Allocator decisions conform to the service contracts
- Multiple sleeves can compete for shared bankroll under one allocator

## References
- `docs/specs/service-contracts-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/system-spec-v1.md`

issues.title: Implement deterministic risk kernel and halt logic
issues.body:
## Outcome
Enforce the hard risk rules from the v1 risk policy.

## Scope
- Encode exposure, drawdown, liquidity, and staleness checks
- Support `approved`, `resized`, `rejected`, and `halted` outcomes
- Trigger stop conditions on stale state, reconciliation failure, or operator pause/flatten

## Acceptance Criteria
- Risk results conform to the `risk_decision` contract
- LLM output cannot override hard limits
- Halt conditions stop new execution immediately

## References
- `docs/specs/risk-policy-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/service-contracts-v1.md`

issues.title: Implement execution engine, heartbeat handling, and reconciliation
issues.body:
## Outcome
Turn approved intents into safe exchange behavior and keep order/account state reconciled.

## Scope
- Translate `execution_intent` into concrete order plans
- Manage order lifecycle, retries, cancel rules, and heartbeats
- Reconcile exchange events back into canonical state

## Acceptance Criteria
- Execution accepts only approved intents
- Heartbeat and cancel behavior are deterministic
- Reconciliation mismatches trigger the correct halt flow
- Order lifecycle events follow the approved contract

## References
- `docs/specs/risk-policy-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/service-contracts-v1.md`

issues.title: [Epic] M3 OpenClaw control plane
issues.body:
## Outcome
Build the operator-facing control plane and the first strategy proposal path on top of the deterministic system.

## Scope
- Slack operator commands
- OpenClaw orchestration boundaries
- Cross-market consistency proposal generation

## Acceptance Criteria
- OpenClaw acts as allocator/coordinator, not direct execution engine
- Operator commands are available in Slack
- Strategy proposals enter `trade-core` via the approved contract

## References
- `docs/specs/system-spec-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/service-contracts-v1.md`

issues.title: Implement Slack operator commands and control workflows
issues.body:
## Outcome
Expose the required operator surface in Slack.

## Scope
- Implement `status`, `why`, `risk`, `pause`, `resume`, `flatten`, `mode`, and `sleeves`
- Route commands through OpenClaw without giving it direct order authority
- Make operator actions visible in the ledger

## Acceptance Criteria
- All required commands work in nonprod
- Pause and flatten produce immediate downstream effects
- Operator actions are logged and traceable

## References
- `docs/specs/system-spec-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/risk-policy-v1.md`

issues.title: Implement cross-market consistency proposal generator
issues.body:
## Outcome
Generate the first live-trading strategy proposals under the v1 scope.

## Scope
- Define internal logic for linked market complexes
- Estimate edge after costs, confidence, holding horizon, and invalidators
- Emit `strategy_proposal` payloads for allocator review

## Acceptance Criteria
- Only cross-market consistency proposals are produced for live trading use
- Proposals contain all required fields from the v1 contract
- Proposal TTL and invalidation logic match the risk policy

## References
- `docs/specs/system-spec-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/service-contracts-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/risk-policy-v1.md`

issues.title: [Epic] M4 Replay, ledger, and paper-trading readiness
issues.body:
## Outcome
Make the system testable, reviewable, and safe to promote from simulation into paper trading.

## Scope
- Decision ledger
- Scorecards and summaries
- Replay harness and promotion tests
- Production release runbook

## Acceptance Criteria
- The team can replay and inspect core decisions deterministically
- Promotion gates from the v1 specs are testable
- Paper mode can be assessed on operational and decision quality grounds

## References
- `docs/specs/system-spec-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/risk-policy-v1.md`

issues.title: Implement decision ledger, scorecards, and daily summaries
issues.body:
## Outcome
Create the audit and learning layer for the system.

## Scope
- Record proposals, decisions, order events, and operator actions
- Produce daily summaries for P&L, exposure, rejects, and health events
- Maintain scorecards by sleeve and market complex

## Acceptance Criteria
- The ledger is immutable by default
- Daily summaries are generated from recorded events
- Scorecards support later promotion and bankroll decisions

## References
- `docs/specs/system-spec-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/service-contracts-v1.md`

issues.title: Build replay harness and promotion test suite
issues.body:
## Outcome
Create the test harness needed to promote safely between modes.

## Scope
- Replay archived market state and recorded decisions
- Validate allocator, risk, and execution behavior deterministically
- Encode promotion checks for `sim` to `paper` and `paper` to `prod`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Replays produce reproducible outputs with identical inputs
- Promotion checks cover the gates in the v1 system spec
- The suite can be run before enabling the next operating mode

## References
- `docs/specs/system-spec-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/risk-policy-v1.md`

issues.title: Prepare production release runbook and beta enablement gate
issues.body:
## Outcome
Document the exact path from paper mode to a live release once Polymarket provides the greenlight.

## Scope
- Define the operator checklist for enabling `prod`
- Record the beta-release dependency on Polymarket approval
- Document rollback, pause, flatten, and incident procedures

## Acceptance Criteria
- The runbook matches the environment and risk specs
- `prod` enablement steps are explicit and privileged
- External platform approval is called out as a blocking dependency

## References
- `docs/specs/environment-matrix-v1.md`
- `docs/specs/risk-policy-v1.md`
